meaning: adj.未予调查的,未作调查研究的
examples: They reported that clouds are an important and heretofore uninvestigated contributor to the climate.
Apparently, this stemmed from uninvestigated reports.
Whether the loss of frontal tissue has any relationship with the robust political opinions for which London cabbies are renowned is an area that remains mercifully uninvestigated.
Rather, they say, it is the opposite: a case of celebrities commanding more attention than many victims of more serious, equally uninvestigated crimes (such as much burglary) receive.
